title: Immediately dangling references and pointers should not be created
description: You can create a temporary object as part of the evaluation of an expression. int getOffset(int i) { int x = std::vector{10, 20, 30}[i] / (i + 1); return x +std::stoi("15"); The code above contains two full expressions (declaration of i and return), each contains a temporary: std::vector{10, 20, 30} is a temporary vector created in-place, and accessed directly. "15" is implicitly converted to temporary std::string{"15"} because std::stoi accepts std::string const& as an argument.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: const int& ref = getValue();  // Dangling reference to temporary
const int* ptr = &getValue(); // Dangling pointer to temporary
auto& vec_elem = std::vector{1, 2, 3}[0]; // Reference to temporary vector element
compliant: int value = getValue(); // Copy the value, not a reference

const std::vector<int> vec{1, 2, 3};
const int& vec_elem = vec[0]; // Reference to persistent object

checks.message: Immediately dangling references and pointers should not be created
